title: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus
summary: CME00010 is a manually keyed University of Michigan encoding of selected matter from William Henry Hulme's 1907 EETS edition: three separately lineated manuscript versions of The Harrowing of Hell and four separately lineated manuscript versions of the strophic Gospel of Nicodemus, with interleaved editorial notes. The XML does not encode the edition's preface, introduction, glossary, index, or outer series matter. The source edition and complete publicly obtainable digital objects for Bodleian MS Digby 86, the Auchinleck manuscript's exposed 701-image project inventory, and Lambeth Sion L40.2/E25 are cached locally; current British Library records do not provide obtainable complete image objects for the remaining four BL witnesses, and Harley MS 2253's former full digital coverage is currently unavailable.
review_notes: The seven DIV2 units are witness texts in a parallel-witness scholarly edition, not seven intrinsic chapters or seven independent abstract works.
Printed pages 22–23 are shared physical surfaces: the final Harrowing columns overlap the first Gospel columns, so the work boundary cannot be represented as a simple page break.
Complete facsimile coverage means the provider's complete publicly exposed physical-object sequence. Work-only leaves are recorded separately in local_copies[].work_portion.
The Auchinleck bundle is complete for the project's 701 public codex and detached-fragment surfaces; it does not assert that covers, spine, edges, or preservation masters are publicly exposed.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00010
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00010.
The compact source header records a 2016 review date and reviewer but no source description, availability statement, or explicit production method.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00010
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text based on selected portions of Hulme's 1907 edition. It preserves the seven witness versions and much local apparatus but omits the preface, introduction, glossary, index, and outer series matter.

entities.label: William Henry Hulme, The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus (1907)

bibliographic.citation: William Henry Hulme, ed., The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus, Early English Text Society, Extra Series 100 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1907), lxx + 150 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 100

physical_description.extent: lxx preliminary pages and 150 numbered pages
notes: The edited Harrowing and Gospel witness texts occupy printed pp. 2–136 in parallel columns; glossary pp. 137–148 and index pp. 149–150 are not encoded in CME00010.

entities.label: Hermann Varnhagen, The Harrowing of Hell (1898)

bibliographic.citation: Hermann Varnhagen, The Harrowing of Hell: Editionis criticae vetustissimi quod sermone anglico conscriptum est dramatis pars prior (Erlangen, 1898), 34 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Harrowing of Hell

physical_description.extent: 1 leaf + 34 pages + 1 leaf, within a university program
notes: Printed pp. 6–21 place diplomatic transcriptions of the three Harrowing witnesses in parallel; pp. 22–34 discuss their relationship. This is not a photographic manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Digby 86
entities.description: Witness O of The Harrowing of Hell, on ff. 119r–120v, within a multilingual miscellany.

notes: Produced in Worcestershire; contains English, Latin, and Old French.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2253
entities.description: Witness L of The Harrowing of Hell, on ff. 55v–56v, copied by the principal Harley scribe in or near Ludlow.

date_statements.label: current catalog date of manuscript part

physical_description.extent: f. 1* + 142 folios, with three unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning and two at the end
notes: The Harrowing is item 7 of part II, ff. 49r–142v; the current component record gives ff. 55v–56v.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)
entities.description: Witness E of The Harrowing of Hell. The text begins and ends imperfectly around damaged or lost leaves; the current project gives the uncertain textual boundary as ff. ?35rb–?37rb or 37va stub, while surviving public images containing the text run from f. 35v through f. 37r.

holding.notes: The current codex contains i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios plus modern endleaves; ten detached folios survive in other repositories.

physical_description.extent: i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios, 14 stubs, modern endleaves, and 10 detached folios in other repositories
notes: Probably produced in London; the current codex is incomplete.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff. 3–114

date_statements.label: current catalog date

physical_description.extent: 258 folios, five unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning, three at the end, and a parchment stub between ff. 213 and 214

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, Sion L40.2/E25

The associated IIIF manifest describes the compound object but contains no canvases, so the complete MediaManager image inventory is bundled instead.

rights.basis: The compound object gives only the terse rights value 'LPL'; Lambeth's current reproduction form requires separate permission for publication and warns that the Library does not own copyright in every holding.
notes: No copyright ownership or open license is inferred from public image delivery.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three Harrowing witnesses and four strophic Gospel witnesses, plus previous reprints
editorial_practices.representation: Separate manuscript texts in parallel columns with variants and changed readings in notes
editorial_practices.description: Hulme states that the volume aims to make all known Middle English versions of the Harrowing and the poetical Gospel accessible, reproducing manuscript texts in parallel columns so far as possible and recording variants from previous reprints.
The preface says manuscript abbreviations are expanded in italics; the XML preserves many corresponding italic spans.
Hulme explicitly says that punctuation is modernized while manuscript readings and capitalization are retained almost without exception.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 1907 volume: lxx + 150 pages plus outer series matter
editorial_practices.representation: Main title, Harrowing part title, seven witness texts, and local apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Direct source comparison shows that CME00010 omits the preface, introduction, prose-Gospel bibliography, glossary, index, and outer matter while retaining printed pp. 2–136 and their interleaved notes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Page-by-page parallel manuscript columns in Hulme's edition
editorial_practices.representation: Seven consecutive DIV2 witness-text divisions with repeated page milestones
editorial_practices.description: The encoding serializes Hulme's parallel witness columns as consecutive divisions. Repeated PB values and shared printed pp. 22–23 preserve the edition's physical overlap without turning witness versions into intrinsic chapters.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00010.xml at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.summary: Provides the repository artifact, identifiers, title matter, seven witness-text divisions, notes, page/leaf milestones, and compact revision history.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual inspection of CME00010.xml against the cached 1907 scan
evidence.locator: 4 DIV1; 7 DIV2; 7,728 L; 586 LG; 431 NOTE; 262 PB; title matter and printed pp. 2–136
evidence.summary: Verifies the exact witness order and XML element counts, shows that parallel columns are serialized as witness divisions, and identifies the noncontiguous selection and omitted volume matter.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00010 item
evidence.summary: Confirms the public item title, 1907 date, subject, stable identifier, encoded text, and item-level public-domain / No Copyright—United States rights assertion.
notes: The U-M display names Oxford University Press as publisher, while the inspected 1907 title page names Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.; the title page controls the edition entity's imprint statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse
evidence.summary: States that the 2016–2018 expansion included Gospel of Nicodemus and that this batch was manually keyed from public-domain editions, with in-house proofreading/editing and basic TEI markup.

evidence.citation: William Henry Hulme, ed., The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus, EETS Extra Series 100 (London, 1907), complete cached scan
evidence.locator: title page; preface pp. v–vi; introduction pp. vii–lxx; edited texts pp. 2–136; glossary pp. 137–148; index pp. 149–150
evidence.summary: Verifies the imprint, extent, edition boundary, all seven manuscript sources and locators, editorial purpose, parallel layout, collation, abbreviation expansion, reading policy, and modernized punctuation.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item middleenglishha00hulmgoog
evidence.summary: Supplies the complete Google-digitized printed-volume surrogate and direct PDF download used for the cached research copy.

evidence.citation: Google digitization notice bound into the Internet Archive scan of Hulme 1907
evidence.summary: Distinguishes Google's view of the underlying public-domain book from modern scan/service conditions, including attribution and use restrictions; it is not an open license.

evidence.citation: PCMEP, The Harrowing of Hell text details
evidence.summary: Identifies the anonymous short-couplet poem, composition range 1200–1250 with suggested date 1240, unknown original dialect, and DIMEV 3070.

evidence.citation: PCMEP, The Strophic Gospel of Nicodemus text details
evidence.summary: Identifies the anonymous northern rhymed rendition, composition range 1300–1350 with suggested date 1340, stanza form, DIMEV 833, four witnesses, current shelfmarks, dates, and folio loci.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Digby 86 catalog and Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 1272–1282 Worcestershire production, 207-leaf extent, multilingual miscellany context, complete 430-canvas object, exact ff. 119r–120v canvases, and CC BY-NC 4.0 photo attribution.

evidence.citation: Inspection of cached complete Digital Bodleian MS Digby 86 bundle
evidence.summary: Verifies covers, inside covers, endleaves, all folios, blanks, adjacent works, and the target's four-canvas location within the complete object.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253 and The Harrowing of Hell component
evidence.summary: Confirms the whole-codex structure and extent, c. 1330–1340 Ludlow-area part, Harrowing item on ff. 55v–56v, physical access route, and currently unavailable full digital coverage.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Adv.MS.19.2.1, Auchinleck manuscript
evidence.summary: Verifies the current and former shelfmarks, c. 1331–1340 likely London production, physical extent, detached fragments, digital surrogate, and physical-access route.

evidence.citation: The Auchinleck Manuscript, ed. David Burnley and Alison Wiggins, National Library of Scotland
evidence.summary: Enumerates the manuscript in codex order, links transcription and public facsimile surfaces, and supports the verified complete 701-image project inventory.

evidence.citation: The Auchinleck Manuscript project, How to refer to this site, Copyright and use
evidence.summary: States that all project materials are copyrighted, provides them for personal use, and prohibits unauthorized commercial use or publication.

evidence.citation: The Auchinleck Manuscript project, The Harrowing of Hell headnote and transcription
evidence.summary: Identifies scribe, verse form, 200-line surviving text, uncertain boundary ff. ?35rb–?37rb or 37va stub, imperfect beginning and ending, at least 75 lost lines, and public images f. 35v–37r (inventory 72–75).

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff. 3–114
evidence.summary: Confirms the current component boundary, first-quarter-15th-century date, Middle English contents, Gospel of Nicodemus on ff. 57v–66v, physical-access route, and absence of a linked digital object.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 4196
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1375–1425 English parchment volume, full extent, Gospel of Nicodemus on ff. 206r–215r, physical-access route, and absence of a linked digital object.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue, Sion L40.2/E25
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 14th–15th-century date, support, extent, binding, multiple hands, Gospel on ff. 13r–38v with fragmentary f. 31, surrounding works, physical/microfilm access, and the linked digital copy.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA compound-book object SionL40.2E25
evidence.summary: Supplies the official 279-image complete book sequence and terse rights value 'LPL'; the compound IIIF manifest itself contains zero canvases.

evidence.citation: Inspection of cached complete Lambeth Sion L40.2/E25 image bundle
evidence.locator: 279 images; Gospel images 31–82; f. 31 fragment at images 67–68
evidence.summary: Verifies upper/lower and inside covers, flyleaves, all folios, blanks, fragments, adjacent works, reused binding leaf, and the work mapping from f. 13r to f. 38v.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library, Application for Permission to Reproduce Images
evidence.summary: Requires separate permission for specified reproduction uses, limits grants to the approved use, requires acknowledgment, and states that Lambeth does not own copyright in all holdings.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 32578
evidence.summary: Confirms the paper volume's 140-folio extent, 15th-century Gospel hand, Nichodemus de passione Christi on ff. 116v–140v, physical-access route, and absence of a linked digital object.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek / Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek, bsb11855722, The Harrowing of Hell (1898)
evidence.summary: Confirms Varnhagen's 1898 Erlangen publication, extent, complete 44-canvas IIIF object, NoC-NC 1.0 statement, and—through provider OCR and image inspection—the diplomatic parallel transcriptions and stemmatic discussion.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-object search for Cotton MS Galba E IX, Harley MS 4196, and Add MS 32578
evidence.summary: Exact current British Library records and targeted searches of institutional web results, Internet Archive, and Google Books found no publicly obtainable complete digital manuscript object for these three witnesses. Harley MS 2253 is recorded separately because full digital coverage exists but is currently unavailable.
notes: This is a dated negative finding, not a claim that no images exist anywhere or that a repository cannot supply reproductions on request.

open_questions.question: How should differing modern line counts and lineation systems for the three Harrowing witnesses be reconciled without erasing editorial differences?
next_steps: Collate Hulme's printed line numbering, the XML L elements, Varnhagen's diplomatic edition, and current manuscript-project counts.
Record any normalization only as an explicit editorial mapping, not as a replacement for source-specific counts.
notes: The XML has O 256, L 248, and E 200 line elements; historical prose summaries and current project records do not all use the same count.

open_questions.question: What exact textual boundary should be assigned to the incomplete Auchinleck Harrowing around the damaged stubs and lost leaves?
next_steps: Consult codicological studies and NLS conservation documentation for the f. 35/37 stub sequence.
Retain the current project's uncertainty as ff. ?35rb–?37rb or 37va stub until stronger evidence resolves it.

open_questions.question: When will Harley MS 2253's full digital coverage return, and can the British Library provide complete digital objects for Cotton MS Galba E IX, Harley MS 4196, and Add MS 32578?
next_steps: Monitor the exact Archives and Manuscripts records and British Library digital-service recovery notices.
Ask the British Library about mediated full-manuscript files or whole-object reproduction orders for all four witnesses.
If only selected images are supplied, mark them partial and do not substitute them for a complete physical-object sequence.

open_questions.question: What project documentation records the exact local decisions that selected title matter and printed pp. 2–136 while omitting the 1907 introduction, glossary, and index?
next_steps: Ask the University of Michigan Digital Content and Collections unit for production notes or source-package documentation for CME00010.
Preserve the observable source comparison separately from any future explanation of why the selection was made.

open_questions.question: What item-level copyright and reuse terms govern the publicly delivered Sion L40.2/E25 compound-book images?
next_steps: Ask Lambeth Palace Library to expand the LUNA rights value 'LPL' for this object and distinguish copyright from contractual reproduction permission.
Continue to treat publication reuse as permission-required until item-level terms are supplied.
